entries.speaker: cael
lines:
Good. Don't hold back. I need you strong enough for
what comes after.

entries.speaker: cael
lines:
You feel that? The weight? That's not me. That's what
I've been carrying since the night I took the Pendulum.

entries.speaker: cael
lines:
That parry. We learned it together. Under Marek. I
remember.

entries.speaker: cael
lines: Lira — your timing is better. You've been practicing.

entries.speaker: cael
lines:
Torren, the spirits are screaming at you to run.
You should listen.

entries.speaker: cael
lines: Sable. Still showing up. I always admired that.

entries.speaker: cael
lines:
Maren. You called it a door. Back at the refuge. You
were right about all of it. And you couldn't stop me anyway.

entries.speaker: cael
lines:
I'm doing this for you. All of it. The machine, the
Convergence — I found a way to end the cycle.

entries.speaker: cael
lines: Remember the cost. Someone has to.

entries.speaker: cael
lines:
I'm doing this for you. Remember that. When it's over,
when you see what I've become — remember I chose this.

His movements are wrong. Too fast. Too sharp. The sword screams
when it swings — not metal on air, but something beneath the
metal, howling.
The grey speaks through him. Not words — feelings given shape.

entries.speaker: cael
lines: ...stop... fighting... let it...

entries.speaker: cael
lines: The machine... close... so close...

The Pallor forces Cael's body forward. His feet leave grooves in
the stone.

Grey mist seeps from Cael's armour. The air tastes of metal and
old grief.

entries.speaker: cael
lines:
I can still hear you. I can still — the machine is
close. Don't stop. Please don't stop.

entries.speaker: cael
lines: ...sorry... can't... control...

He should have fallen. The Pallor energy flares — grey light
pours from every crack in his armour. He stands. Not because he
wants to. Because the Pallor won't let go.

entries.speaker: maren
lines: He should have fallen. The Pallor won't let him.

entries.speaker: edren
lines: One more push. For him. Not against him. For him.

Cael falls. Not dramatically. He crumples — the way a man does
when he has nothing left. The sword clatters on stone. His hands
open. His eyes close.
And then the machine wakes up.
(If Edren uses Steadfast Resolve during the fight.)

entries.speaker: edren
lines:
I learned this at the monastery. I'm not leaving you,
Cael. Not again.
(If Lira uses a healing ability on the party.)

entries.speaker: lira
lines:
The Pallor's grip is fluctuating. He's still in there.
I can see it in the energy patterns.
(If Torren uses Rootsong.)

entries.speaker: torren
lines:
The spirits remember him. They're singing for him.
Not against him.
(If Sable is in the party.)

entries.speaker: sable
lines:
This is the worst fight I've ever been in. And I've
been in a lot of fights.

entries.speaker: sable
lines:
He said please. Cael never says please. He's still
in there.
(If Maren uses Pallor Sight.)

entries.speaker: maren
lines:
I can see the Pallor's grip. It's wrapped around his
heart. But there's a gap. He's holding it open. For us.
